[2023-02-03] Modulus of elasticity measures stiffness of materials
nodes.description: The modulus of elasticity, also known as Young's modulus, is a measure of the stiffness or resistance to deformation of a material, defined as the ratio of stress to strain within the proportional limit of the material.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Higher modulus of elasticity indicates stiffer materials
nodes.description: A higher modulus of elasticity indicates that the material is stiffer and less likely to deform under load, while a lower modulus indicates that the material is more flexible and more likely to deform.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Modulus of elasticity is important in engineering and materials science
nodes.description: The modulus of elasticity is an important material property in engineering and materials science, used to design and analyze structures and components subjected to external forces.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Moment of inertia is a geometric property of cross-sectional shapes
nodes.description: The moment of inertia, also known as the second moment of area, describes a cross-sectional shape's resistance to bending or torsion.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Moment of inertia formula varies by cross-sectional shape
nodes.description: The formula for the moment of inertia I depends on the specific cross-sectional shape of the object, with examples including rectangular, circular, hollow circular, and triangular cross-sections.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Stress-strain diagram represents material behavior under loading
nodes.description: A stress-strain diagram is a graphical representation of the relationship between stress and strain in a material, divided into elastic, plastic, and failure regions.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Percent elongation measures material ductility
nodes.description: Percent elongation is a measure of a material's ductility, defined as the increase in length after a tensile test, expressed as a percentage of the original length.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Poisson's ratio measures lateral deformation in materials
nodes.description: Poisson's ratio is defined as the negative ratio of transverse strain to axial strain, describing how a material deforms laterally when subjected to axial stress.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Poisson's ratio can be found using tensile testing
nodes.description: One method to find Poisson's ratio is to perform a tensile test, measuring longitudinal and lateral strains to calculate the ratio.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Poisson's ratio can be found using flexural testing
nodes.description: Another method to find Poisson's ratio is through flexural testing, where a beam-shaped specimen is loaded and its deflection is measured to calculate strains.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Poisson's ratio can be found using ultrasonic testing
nodes.description: Poisson's ratio can also be determined using ultrasonic testing methods by measuring the velocities of longitudinal and transverse waves in the material.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Heidegger's theory emphasizes critical reflection and questioning assumptions about reality

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Heidegger's theory provides a framework for critical reflection on our understanding of reality

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Basel's single trip public transportation price is CHF 3.60 for adults
nodes.description: A single trip within Basel city limits on a tram or bus costs CHF 3.60 for adults and CHF 2.00 for children, with a day pass costing CHF 9.00 for adults and CHF 4.50 for children.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Basel's public transportation prices are generally similar to Zurich
nodes.description: Compared to Zurich, where a single trip costs CHF 4.30 for adults and CHF 2.30 for children, Basel's prices are similar, but Zurich has more expensive options for long-distance travel.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Basel's public transportation prices are lower than Geneva
nodes.description: In comparison to Geneva, where a single trip costs CHF 3.00 for adults and CHF 1.50 for children, Basel's prices are higher for adults but lower for children, with Geneva offering student and youth discounts.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Public transportation in Basel can get busy during peak hours
nodes.description: Like most major cities, public transportation in Basel can become crowded during rush hour in the morning and late afternoon, particularly on popular routes connecting residential areas with city centers or train stations.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Tips for avoiding overcrowded trains or trams in Basel include avoiding peak hours
nodes.description: To avoid overcrowded trains and trams, users can: 1. Avoid peak hours if possible, 2. Use alternative modes of transportation, 3. Avoid popular routes or train stations, 4. Plan trips in advance, and 5. Board at previous stations for better seating chances.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Tickets for public transportation in Basel can be purchased in several ways
nodes.description: In Basel, tickets can be bought at ticket machines, ticket offices, or online, with many machines accepting both cash and credit cards.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Online ticket purchases for Basel's public transportation are available through BVB
nodes.description: Users can buy single tickets, day passes, and other types of tickets online from the BVB (Basler Verkehrs-Betriebe) website, receiving tickets by email or download link.

[2023-02-03] User explained best practices for password policy and guidelines
nodes.description: User provided a comprehensive overview of password policy best practices, including password complexity, length, expiration, history, two-factor authentication, training, storage, and regular auditing.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Password policy should require complex passwords
nodes.description: Password complexity should include a mix of upper and lower case letters, numbers, and special characters.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Passwords should be at least 8 characters long
nodes.description: Longer passwords are generally more secure.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Passwords should expire after a set period of time
nodes.description: For example, passwords should expire after 90 days to encourage regular changes and enhance security.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Users should not be allowed to reuse old passwords
nodes.description: This practice helps to prevent password guessing attacks.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Two-factor authentication should be required for all users
nodes.description: This can include something the user knows (like a password) and something the user has (like a security token).

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Users should receive regular training on password security best practices
nodes.description: Training should include tips on creating secure passwords, recognizing phishing attacks, and avoiding common password mistakes.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Passwords should be stored securely using strong encryption
nodes.description: This practice helps to prevent unauthorized access to the password database.

nodes.title: [2023-02-03] Password policy should be audited regularly
nodes.description: Regular audits ensure that the password policy remains effective and up to date.
